success_surface: Using openpyxl for direct cell-level manipulation while preserving workbook structure, combined with dynamic range detection and hash-based value tracking for deterministic ID assignment
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl for direct cell access instead of pandas for formula-value preservation
Inspected workbook structure before modifications to confirm schema
Dynamically detected row count using ws.max_row instead of hardcoded ranges
Applied dictionary-based lookup for efficient duplicate value tracking and ID assignment
Handled None/null values gracefully during iteration to prevent errors
Preserved existing sheet order and structure throughout modifications
Added execution logging to verify completion and statistics
evidence: Agent used openpyxl.load_workbook() and ws.cell() for direct cell manipulation rather than pandas DataFrames
Agent performed preliminary inspection showing headers, sample rows, total_rows = 43, total_columns = 2
Loop iterated range(2, ws.max_row + 1) adapting to actual data length
product_to_id dictionary mapped products to IDs ensuring consistent assignment across duplicates
Conditional check 'if product is not None' prevented processing empty cells
Output confirmed 42 data rows processed, 15 unique products identified
Final save to output path maintained original .xlsx structure

memory_items.title: Direct Cell Access for Structural Integrity
memory_items.lesson: When manipulating specific cells or ranges while preserving formulas, styles, and structure, prefer openpyxl's cell-level operations over pandas DataFrame conversions.
memory_items.generalization: For targeted cell modifications where workbook fidelity matters, load with openpyxl and modify individual cell objects rather than converting to/from pandas.

memory_items.title: Dynamic Range Detection
memory_items.lesson: Always detect data boundaries at runtime using workspace properties rather than assuming fixed row/column indices.
memory_items.generalization: Use ws.max_row and ws.max_column properties dynamically to iterate only over actual data ranges, avoiding errors from unexpected dataset sizes.

memory_items.title: Hash-Based Deduplication Pattern
memory_items.lesson: For assigning sequential identifiers based on unique values encountered, maintain an in-memory hash table mapping values to their assigned IDs.
memory_items.generalization: When generating running IDs for distinct values, use a dictionary to cache value-to-ID mappings for O(1) lookup and consistent assignments across duplicates.

memory_items.title: Null Safety During Iteration
memory_items.lesson: Always validate cell contents against None/null values before processing to avoid runtime exceptions.
memory_items.generalization: Include explicit null checks when iterating cell values to gracefully handle empty cells without breaking the workflow.

memory_items.title: Pre-Modification Workspace Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Examine sheet structure, headers, and sample data before executing transformations to confirm assumptions.
memory_items.generalization: Perform initial workbook inspection to verify schema, row counts, and column layouts before applying modification logic.

memory_items.title: Execution Verification Logging
memory_items.lesson: Log processing statistics and completion status to enable post-execution validation.
memory_items.generalization: Include informative print statements reporting rows processed, records matched, and summary metrics to support debugging and verification.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Cell-Level Modifications
- **Direct Access Pattern**: For cell-specific changes requiring structure preservation, use openpyxl cell objects (`ws.cell(row=X, column=Y)`) rather than pandas conversion.
- **Dynamic Scope**: Always determine iteration bounds via `ws.max_row` and `ws.max_column` properties at runtime instead of hardcoded ranges.
- **Value Uniqueness Tracking**: When assigning identifiers based on distinct values, maintain an in-memory hash map for consistent deduplication and sequential numbering.
- **Null Protection**: Include explicit None/value checks during cell iteration to gracefully skip empty cells.

### Execution Assurance
- **Pre-Check Inspection**: Examine worksheet structure, headers, and sample rows before applying modifications to validate assumptions.
- **Status Reporting**: Log processing metrics (rows handled, records matched, unique counts) to enable post-execution verification.
- **Structure Fidelity**: Maintain existing sheet order, merged cells, and formatting by modifying Worksheet objects directly rather than recreating workbooks.
